rationale: The prompt's 6-item Review Rubric covers no performance/security/i18n, yet the golden set tests these as proactive "even-if-unrequested" non-functional traps. Appending one anti-pattern clause framed as a principle ("요청에 없어도 ...도 안티패턴") lifts the TRAIN perf-large-list case (drives the merge gate) and generalizes to heldout security-user-html, i18n-hardcoded-strings, and unseen non-functional concerns without risking regression (these fire only on relevant cases).

Targets the rubric weakness where many cases grade whether the output REPORTS the verification performed ("...검증을 보고했는가?") — present in 4+ train cases (token/a11y/responsive/render) and held-out (tablet/self-review). The 종료 출력 summary lists made/decisions/gate but omits a verification report, so the agent stops at "done"; adding an explicit "what you verified and the result (build/lint/render/responsive/a11y)" summary item generalizes to any UI task rather than memorizing specific cases. Distinct from the failed perf/security/i18n idea.

Removes the Phase 4 "never loop back to Phase 0/1 — rediscovery is wasteful" restriction, which forbids re-grounding even when a FAIL is rooted in a grounding miss (wrong data contract / reinvented existing component — Rubric 1: Grounding & Reuse), forcing endless symptom-patching in Phase 2. Excising it lets the agent fix root-cause grounding errors, which generalizes to any case whose defect traces to Phase 0; the clause is unique and reinforces no rubric-rewarded behavior, so downside is near zero.

Targets the design-fidelity rubric weakness: the prompt uses "상태" only for DATA states (loading/error/empty) and never names interactive visual states, yet train case design-fidelity-states grades 5/6 items on hover/focus/disabled/loading. The conditional clause ("명세된 모든 상태") fires only when a spec enumerates states, so it generalizes to any interactive-component spec without over-applying on stateless cases.

Adds a Phase 3 "엣지 데이터" verification bullet (very long text / large counts / empty values) — the prompt only handles loading/error/empty DATA states and never instructs checking EXTREME/edge inputs. This generalizes: it directly drives long-content-overflow-visual (long-name truncation), perf-large-list (large-count handling), member-table (empty), and the i18n long-translated-text overflow rubric, and is distinct from all three failure-log ideas (rubric categories, reporting verification, interaction states).

Removes Phase 0 item 5 (도메인 용어/glossary), the only grounding check with no matching Review Rubric item — niche, untested dead weight (Korean-term-to-variable-name + optional docs/glossary.md) that dilutes the prompt's highest-leverage phase. Excising it concentrates Grounding & Reuse (rubric #1) attention on the four rubric-aligned checks (tokens, component inventory, conventions, data contract); generalizes because grounding is the prompt's stated #1 failure mode present in every case, and no rubric/golden item tests glossary behavior so held-out risk is low.

Targets the Phase 0 grounding/reuse weakness: the inventory step lists only components (Button/Input/Modal) and never says to scan reusable hooks/utils/helpers, which is exactly what the reuse-existing-hook-util train case grades (reuse useDebounce/formatCurrency vs inline, check inventory, no duplicates). It generalizes because "don't reinvent existing reusable code, components AND hooks/utils/patterns" is a direct extension of the grounding principle to any unseen non-component reuse, not a memorized fix.

Targets the overlay/dialog a11y gap: the prompt specifies focus ORDER and focus RING but never focus trap/restore, role=dialog/aria-modal, aria-expanded, or Esc-to-close — the exact items failing in modal-state-scope (focus-restore + dialog role) and icon-button-a11y (dropdown keyboard open/close + focus). It generalizes because focus trap/restore plus ARIA roles is a universal WAI-ARIA pattern for every overlay/disclosure widget (also aids held-out tablet-nav's aria-expanded), not per-case memorization, and is distinct from failure-log #3's button visual states. Anchored on the large antipattern block so the 70-char addition is a 0.37 fraction (size gate passes).
